name: Gauged L_mu - L_tau symmetry
summary: Gauges the anomaly-free difference of muon and tau lepton numbers (L_mu - L_tau). The associated Z' boson couples only to muons, taus, and the second/third-generation neutrinos at tree level (anomaly-free within SM particle content). Distinguishes lepton flavours and provides natural contributions to (g-2)_mu without conflicting with electron-sector precision tests. Heavily studied as a leading framework for the muon-magnetic-moment anomaly.

addresses_puzzles.note: Z' loop contributes a positive Delta a_mu ~ 250e-11 for M_Z' ~ 10-100 MeV and g' ~ 10^-4 - 10^-3, matching the WP2020-vs-experiment anomaly. Narrow surviving parameter space after neutrino-trident (CCFR) bounds and Borexino solar-neutrino constraints.

Predicts a mu-tau-symmetric neutrino mass matrix at leading order (sin^2 theta_23 = 1/2, theta_13 = 0 before breaking); deviations from maximal atmospheric mixing reflect L_mu - L_tau-breaking corrections.

A gauged U(1)_{L_mu - L_tau} with M_Z' ~ 10-100 MeV and g' ~ 10^-3 contributes to muonic hydrogen Lamb shift but not electronic, naturally producing the muonic-vs-electronic r_p discrepancy (Pospelov 2010, Batell-McKeen-Pospelov 2011).

touches_benchmarks.note: Z' loop contribution can saturate Delta a_mu ~ 250e-11; targeted by direct searches at NA64-mu (CERN), Mu3e, FASER.
Predicts narrow window of long-range neutrino-matter interactions detectable at DUNE and JUNO.

new_scales: M_Z' ~ 10 MeV - few GeV (for g-2_mu fit)
introduces.note: Z' mass M_Z' and gauge coupling g'.

history: He, Joshi, Lew, Volkas (1991), 'Simplest Z' model', Phys. Rev. D 44, 2118. Revived in 2010s as a flagship g-2_mu interpretation. Heeck-Rodejohann (2011, arXiv:1107.5238) systematic phenomenology. NA64-mu, FASER, MuSIC actively searching the surviving parameter window.

references.citation: He, Joshi, Lew, Volkas, 'Simplest Z' model', Phys. Rev. D 44, 2118 (1991)
